# Transcrição: VT System_3

- **Fonte:** VT System_3
- **Duração:** 01:58
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:58]

Here's a little trick that I use. Basic building block number three, this is how you expand money. Spend 95% of your time on income and only 5% of your time on expenses. If you take out your financial statement, take out your income statement, you have one. Every family hasn't. You know, the household has an income statement. Money came in, money went out. The line for income to show you how powerful income is, the lines for income are about this many and the expenses are that many. Do you understand? There's this many more expenses, that's how powerful income is. You could have enough income to overwhelm the expenses and that's the point. I want to spend 95% of my time on this number, the little mighty number, mighty mouse, the income. I want to spend 95% of my time when I'm working a budget, when I'm working in a department, a division, a financial statement for a company. If I'm doing a financial statement for all my apartments, okay, we'll do, I don't know, we got 4,000 apartments that do about $4 million a month in income. I'm going to spend all my time not on the millions of dollars we spend on expenses, taxes, property insurance, repairs, roofs, paint, bad credit, all the problems. There's pages and pages of them. I spend 95% of my time on the thing I can control. Income, I spend 95% of my time on my household budget on how me and my wife and the kids can produce more income, not on all those items. I spend 5% of my time on expenses, this is what I do. I start on my expenses. What can we get rid of? Do we need to get rid of anything and then I spend the rest of my time on income? Most people have this in reverse. Most people spend 98% of their time or 99% of their time on their expenses and maybe 1% of their time on their income. You want to be in the 1%? Flip that. Spend 95% of your time on income and 5% of your time on the expenses.
